methodsAttempted.query: site:ucy.ac.cy University of Cyprus AI policy artificial intelligence students teaching assessment generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official UCY AI website pages, UCY AI Guidelines PDFs, and lower-specificity research/event results.

Verified official UCY pages on www.ucy.ac.cy, including the AI hub and UCY Guidelines section. Direct local curl requests to ucy.ac.cy were blocked by Cloudflare, so successful extraction used Firecrawl for public official HTML pages.

methodsAttempted.query: UCY Guidelines AI citation requirements input responsibility usage instructions
methodsAttempted.notes: Official UCY Guidelines subpages provided specific evidence for syllabus instructions, citation transparency, and data-safety expectations.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found UCY's central AI website, a UCY Guidelines page stating Senate adoption of AI guidelines, and official guideline subpages for teaching instructions, citation requirements, and data safety. Official PDFs were discovered but Firecrawl PDF extraction timed out and direct local HTTP fetch was blocked by Cloudflare, so the staged claims rely on the official HTML pages that exposed equivalent guideline excerpts.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Cyprus maintains official AI Guidelines for course instructors, students, and researchers, and the UCY Guidelines page states that guidelines were adopted by Senate decision 14/2025/SENATE on 11 June 2025.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UCY's recommended syllabus statement says students are required to comply with the UCY AI Guidelines and academic-integrity principles, and that improper AI-tool use may result in disciplinary and other consequences.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UCY instructor guidance says instructors should provide clear, specific guidelines for permitted AI-tool use in course syllabi and graded assignment or exam instructions, and explicitly state when AI use is not permitted in graded coursework.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UCY citation guidance says substantial AI-assisted work must be acknowledged or cited properly when permitted, while minimal-risk uses may not need explicit declaration unless a specific context requires it.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UCY input-safety guidance says users must exercise great caution with AI-tool inputs and avoid entering personal, confidential, unpublished research, or copyrighted content.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UCY research-facing AI guidance tells researchers to anonymize data before using AI for analysis and to confirm approval for AI use involving sensitive data.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Instructors should also provide clear, specific guidelines for the permitted use of AI tools in the course syllabus and in the instructions of graded assignments or exams, ensuring that the guidance aligns with course objectives, learning outcomes, and the University's academic integrity standards. If AI use is not permitted in graded coursework, this must be explicitly stated in the assignment instructions.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim supported by the official UCY Guidelines page; not marked canonical.
Approved because the claim uses the source's own required/required-to-comply language and notes consequences without overextending beyond the UCY AI Guidelines context.
Approved as teaching guidance for instructors; wording preserves should/must distinctions from the source.
Approved as academic-integrity/citation guidance with a condition that substantial AI use is cited or acknowledged when permitted.
Approved as privacy/data-handling guidance supported by substantive source text.
Approved as research-facing AI data-safety guidance; no claim is made about all research governance procedures.

artifacts.title: University of Cyprus AI Guidelines Staged Review
artifacts.summary: The University of Cyprus has an official AI website and UCY Guidelines page that states Senate adoption of AI Guidelines for ethical AI use in teaching and research. Official guideline subpages provide narrower evidence on instructor syllabus and assessment instructions, student compliance and academic integrity, citation transparency, and safe handling of personal, confidential, sensitive, unpublished research, and copyrighted data in AI tools.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
OpenClaw staged candidates are not canonical public claims and require downstream review before publication.
Official UCY PDF sources were discovered but not used as evidence because Firecrawl extraction timed out and direct local HTTP fetches were blocked by Cloudflare; no bypass was attempted.
Event/news items and research-project pages were excluded from the claim set because they do not establish standing university AI policy.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
